data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style project:

**Legacy patterns identified:**

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` on `<Project>` | Replace with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit via SDK |
| 4 | `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults | SDK provides defaults |
| 5 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 6 | `FileAlignment` | Default (512), not needed |
| 7 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Not needed for modern TFMs |
| 8 | `Deterministic` | True by default in SDK |
| 9 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK defaults match these |
| 10 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Automatic globbing includes all `*.cs` |
| 11 | `<Reference Include="System">` etc. | Implicit framework references |
| 12 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Properties go in `.csproj`; auto-generated |
| 13 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Use `TargetFramework` short name |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** — handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** — handled automatically by SDK
4. **Configuration/Platform property groups with defaults** — SDK provides sensible defaults


5. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects** — unnecessary in SDK-style, which handles these automatically or makes them irrelevant for modern .NET
6. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style uses globbing to automatically include all *.cs files
7. **Framework references and AssemblyInfo.cs properties** — these are now implicit or can be moved to project properties in the SDK format
8. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** — I need to convert this to the modern `TargetFramework` property using `net472`, or better yet, upgrade to `net6.0` or `net8.0` for a more current target.

Now I'm going to update the project file to use the SDK-style format.

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines to 13 lines. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **47 → 13 lines** — eliminated all boilerplate
- **Deleted `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — metadata now lives in `<PropertyGroup>` (auto-generated at build)
- **No explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK globbing auto-includes all `*.cs` files
- **No framework references** — implicit for `net472`
- **No Debug/Release configs** — SDK defaults already define `DEBUG`/`TRACE` constants, optimization, and output paths
- **`OutputType` omitted** — `Library` is the SDK default

The target framework stays at `net472` to preserve compatibility. To further modernize, you could retarget to `net8.0` or `net9.0`.
